Title: MemorySmith.Agent — Three Bounded Contexts
Content:
The agent is split into three bounded contexts that communicate only through interfaces:

1. Agent Core (Agent.Core, Agent.Planning, Agent.Tools, Agent.Personality): the 'brain'. IAgent, IGoal, IPlan, IMemoryGateway, ITool, IWorldAdapter, IPlanner, IGoalFactory.

2. MemorySmith Knowledge (Agent.Memory): long-term memory. RestMemoryGateway implements IMemoryGateway via GET /api/search and GET/POST/PUT /api/pages on a live MemorySmith instance.

3. World Adapters (Agent.World.Minecraft + MineflayerAdapter/): Minecraft-specific code. MinecraftAdapter (C#) spawns index.js (Node.js) as a subprocess, connects via WebSocket on WS_PORT (default 3000), and forwards ActionData commands. The WebSocket protocol: C# sends {action, arguments}; Node.js sends {event, ...payload}.

WebUI.Blazor is the host: it owns the DI container, REST API, and AgentBackgroundService (IHostedService). Program.cs registers all services when Agent:Enabled=true.
